title: Missouri Revised Statutes В§ 491.725 - Citation of law вҖ” definitions вҖ” applicability
text:
491.725.В В Citation of law вҖ” definitions вҖ” applicability. вҖ” 1.В В This section shall be known and may be cited as the "Child Witness Protection Act".
вҖғВ 2.В В As used in this section, the following terms shall mean:
вҖғВ (1)В В "Child", a person fourteen years of age or under, or at the discretion of the court, a person fifteen to seventeen years of age, who is a witness in any judicial proceeding under chapter 452 or 453, or the alleged victim or witness in any judicial proceeding under chapter 455, 565, 566, or 568.В В The court shall make written findings on the record when a child fifteen to seventeen years of age is included under this subdivision.В В "Victim" or "witness" shall not include any child accused of committing a felony; however, these terms may, in the court's discretion, include:
вҖғВ (a)В В A child where such child's participation in a felony appears to have been induced, coerced, or unwilling; or
вҖғВ (b)В В A child who has participated in the felony, but who has subsequently and voluntarily agreed to testify on behalf of the state;
вҖғВ (2)В В "Support person", an adult, designated by the court to serve as a support person, who is known to the child victim or witness and who has no direct legal or pecuniary interest in the outcome of the judicial proceeding.
вҖғВ 3.В В In order to facilitate testimony that is fair and accurate, for the benefit of all parties, and in order to protect all parties from the risks of a child becoming confused while testifying in a judicial proceeding, the following child witness protection act shall apply to all children testifying in court:
вҖғВ (1)В В Whether at a competency hearing or trial itself, the judge shall ensure that any oath that is required of a child shall be administered in such a manner that the child may fully understand his or her duty to tell the truth;
вҖғВ (2)В В The court shall take care to ensure that questions are stated in a form which is appropriate to the age of the child.В В The court shall explain to the child that if he or she does not understand a question, the child has the right to say that he or she does not understand the question and to have the question restated in a form that the child does understand;
вҖғВ (3)В В In the court's discretion, the taking of testimony from a child victim or witness may be limited in duration or limited to normal school hours.В В The court may order a recess when the energy, comfort, or attention span of the child warrants;
вҖғВ (4)В В Upon motion made by the child, his or her representative, or any party to the judicial proceeding, at least thirty days in advance of the judicial proceeding, the court may allow the child to have a toy, blanket, or similar item in his or her possession while testifying, but such item shall only be allowed if:
вҖғВ (a)В В All parties agree; or
вҖғВ (b)В В If the movant shows the court by a preponderance of evidence that:
вҖғВ a.В В The child in question cannot reliably testify without the item in his or her possession; and
вҖғВ b.В В Allowing the item is not likely to prejudice the trier of fact in hearing and evaluating the child's testimony;
вҖғВ (5)В В Upon motion made by the child, his or her representative, or any party to the judicial proceeding, at least thirty days in advance of the judicial proceeding, the court may designate a support person, who shall be present in the courtroom, in view of the child witness.В В The court may allow the support person to remain in close proximity to the child during the child's testimony, but such action shall only be allowed if:
вҖғВ (a)В В All parties agree; or
вҖғВ (b)В В If the movant shows the court by a preponderance of the evidence that:
вҖғВ a.В В The child in question cannot reliably testify without the support person in close proximity during the testimony; and
вҖғВ b.В В Allowing the support person to be in close proximity to the child during testimony is not likely to prejudice the trier of fact in hearing and evaluating the child's testimony.В В ВӯВӯВӯ
вҖғВ (6)В В The court shall prevent intimidation or harassment of the child witness by the parties or their attorneys.В В Insofar as it is consistent with the constitutional rights of the parties to confront and cross-examine adverse witnesses, the judge may rephrase any questions in order to prevent any such intimidation or harassment; and
вҖғВ (7)В В Upon its own motion or the motion of any party to the judicial proceeding, at least thirty days in advance of the judicial proceeding, the court may order such accommodations as are appropriate under the circumstances to ensure the comfort of the child victim or witness, including the following measures:
вҖғВ (a)В В Adjusting the layout of the courtroom;
вҖғВ (b)В В Conducting the proceedings outside the normal courtroom; or
вҖғВ (c)В В Relaxing the formalities of the proceedings; ВӯВӯВӯ
(L. 2009 H.B. 863)
